compiled_state.title: [check-tickets-boundary]: BASH_SOURCE resolves to temp path under language:script mode -> docs/ path exclusion silently fails
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

When check-tickets-boundary.sh runs as a pre-commit hook with `language: script` in .pre-commit-config.yaml, the hook is invoked via a temp directory symlink rather than its real path. `BASH_SOURCE[0]` resolves to a temp path, so the `_PLUGIN_GIT_PATH` computation (derived from BASH_SOURCE relative to git toplevel) produces an incorrect value or fails entirely. The downstream effect is that path exclusions using `_PLUGIN_GIT_PATH` — specifically the `plugins/dso/docs/` exclusion for documentation files — silently fail to match, causing legitimate doc references to be flagged as violations.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Stage a file under `plugins/dso/docs/` that references an absorbed script name (e.g., `sprint-next-batch.sh` in a migration note)
2. Run `git commit` — pre-commit triggers check-tickets-boundary.sh via language:script mode
3. Observe: hook rejects the file despite it being in the docs/ exclusion path

## Observed Result

The docs/ path exclusion condition evaluates false because `_PLUGIN_GIT_PATH` is wrong. Files that should pass are rejected.

## Expected Result

Files under `plugins/dso/docs/` with absorbed script name references should exit 0 (not be flagged).

## Workaround Applied

Added `# tickets-boundary-ok` suppression annotations to affected lines in `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md`. This is a per-line workaround, not a structural fix.

## Root Cause

The hook uses `BASH_SOURCE[0]` to derive its own location for computing `_PLUGIN_GIT_PATH`. Under pre-commit `language: script`, the hook is invoked through a temp shim path rather than the real file path. The fix should use `readlink -f` or another resolution strategy that is robust to the symlink indirection introduced by pre-commit's script execution environment.
